home.faq.a1: No. NurseNest is an independent educational platform. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to NCLEX, NCSBN, CNO, or any nursing or allied health regulatory body. All content is developed independently by healthcare professionals and is designed to align with the content domains and cognitive patterns assessed on licensure and certification examinations.
home.faq.a10: Yes. All NurseNest content is developed and reviewed by healthcare professionals with clinical and educational expertise. Lessons, practice questions, and case simulations are created to reflect current evidence-based practice guidelines and are designed to align with the content domains assessed on nursing, NP, and allied health certification examinations. Content is regularly updated to maintain clinical accuracy and relevance.
home.faq.a11: Yes. NurseNest includes dedicated exam prep for allied health professions including respiratory therapy (RRT), paramedics (PCP/ACP/NREMT), medical laboratory technologists (MLT), pharmacy technicians, diagnostic imaging technologists, social workers (ASWB), psychotherapists (CRPO), occupational therapists (NBCOT), and physical therapists. Each profession has tailored practice questions, study resources, and career guides.
home.faq.a12: Yes. NurseNest offers comprehensive nurse practitioner certification exam prep covering AANP, ANCC, FNP-BC, AGPCNP-BC, AGACNP-BC, PMHNP-BC, PNP-BC, NNP-BC, ENP-C, and CNM. NP content includes advanced-level practice questions, molecular-level pathophysiology lessons, and diagnostic reasoning exercises designed for the higher clinical complexity expected on NP certification exams.
home.faq.a13: The New Graduate Hub provides clinical survival guides, first-year-on-the-floor resources, certification pathway planning, resume and interview tools, and burnout prevention resources. It
home.faq.a14: NurseNest supports preparation for nursing specialty certifications including CCRN (Critical Care), CEN (Emergency), PCCN (Progressive Care), and others. The platform offers clinical specialty guides covering ICU, NICU, trauma, med-surg, mental health, orthopedics, nephrology, and palliative care nursing — each with targeted practice questions and clinical pearls.
home.faq.a2: NurseNest questions are designed to mirror the clinical reasoning and cognitive patterns tested on healthcare licensure and certification examinations. They are not actual exam questions, but they target the same thinking skills, content domains, and clinical judgment frameworks that examiners use to assess readiness for practice. This includes Next Generation-style case studies and clinical judgment scenarios for nursing, as well as discipline-specific question formats for allied health professions.
home.faq.a3: Absolutely. Many nursing and allied health students use NurseNest alongside their coursework to reinforce pathophysiology, pharmacology, and clinical reasoning skills. The structured progression from foundational concepts to advanced case analysis is designed to complement how healthcare programs build knowledge across semesters, making it valuable from your first year through graduation.
home.faq.a4: Traditional question banks give you a question, four options, and the correct answer. NurseNest provides deep rationales explaining why the correct answer is right and why each distractor is wrong. It connects questions to pathophysiology lessons, includes interactive case simulations with branching outcomes, and tracks your performance across body systems to target your weak areas. The goal is to build clinical reasoning, not just test recall.
home.faq.a5: Yes. NurseNest supports exam preparation for all major nursing licensure and certification examinations, including NCLEX-RN, NCLEX-PN, REX-PN, and all NP certification exams: AANP, ANCC, FNP-BC, AGPCNP-BC, AGACNP-BC, PMHNP-BC, PNP-BC, NNP-BC, ENP-C, and CNM. The platform also covers allied health certifications including RRT, NREMT, MLT, PTCB, NBCOT, and ASWB exams. Whether you
home.faq.a6: The free plan includes Pre-Nursing Foundations modules, interactive anatomy and physiology diagrams, Clinical Clarity explanations, access to the lesson library for browsing, limited practice questions, and up to 50 flashcards across your decks. No credit card is required to start. Premium plans unlock the full question bank, clinical case simulations, unlimited flashcard decks with Learn and Test modes, med math tools, performance analytics, and all 10 language translations.
home.faq.a6b: Learn Mode uses spaced retry to help you master every card. Cards you miss are automatically re-queued until you get them right. Test Mode is a timed assessment where you go through the entire deck once, with a detailed report card at the end showing your accuracy, time spent, and which cards you need to review. Both modes include keyboard shortcuts for faster studying. You can also create your own decks and import cards via CSV to quickly build study sets from your lecture notes.
home.faq.a7: Yes. All subscriptions can be cancelled at any time through your profile page. You retain access to premium features until the end of your current billing period. There are no cancellation fees or long-term commitments.
home.faq.a8: Yes. NurseNest includes question formats designed to mirror the cognitive patterns tested on Next Generation nursing licensure examinations, including case studies with multiple response points, drag-and-drop prioritization, highlight-the-evidence items, and clinical judgment scenarios that require you to recognize cues, analyze information, and evaluate outcomes. These formats are integrated throughout the question bank and clinical simulations.
home.faq.a9suffix: practice questions spanning nursing, NP, and allied health content areas including medical-surgical, pharmacology, maternal-newborn, pediatrics, mental health, critical care, respiratory therapy, paramedicine, and clinical prioritization. New questions are added every week to ensure comprehensive and growing coverage. Each question includes a detailed rationale explaining the clinical reasoning behind the correct answer.

home.study.casesDesc: Work through branching patient scenarios for conditions like sepsis, myocardial infarction, diabetic ketoacidosis, and stroke. Each simulation follows a consequence-based decision pathway, allowing you to practice the clinical judgment process in a risk-free environment before exam day.
home.study.criticalDesc: Develop rapid assessment skills for shock recognition, respiratory failure patterns, neurological deterioration, sepsis identification, and metabolic emergencies like DKA and HHS. Lessons are structured around the clinical judgment process used in acute care settings and tested on licensing examinations.
home.study.heading: What You Can Study on NurseNest
home.study.labDesc: Go beyond memorizing normal ranges. Learn to interpret abnormal lab clusters, analyze arterial blood gases, recognize electrolyte emergencies, and connect diagnostic findings to immediate nursing actions. Each lesson builds the cluster-based reasoning that exam questions demand.
home.study.maternalDesc: Comprehensive coverage of pregnancy complications, labor and delivery assessment, postpartum care, neonatal danger signs, and pediatric conditions across developmental stages. Lessons emphasize the critical thinking required to differentiate normal from abnormal findings in obstetric and pediatric clinical scenarios.
home.study.medsurgDesc: Master cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological, gastrointestinal, renal, and endocrine pathophysiology through lessons designed to align with exam content domains. Each module connects disease mechanisms to the clinical prioritization and acute deterioration patterns that licensing examinations assess most heavily.
home.study.mentalDesc: Explore psychiatric conditions, therapeutic communication techniques, crisis intervention protocols, and psychopharmacology through a clinical reasoning lens. Content addresses the cognitive traps that examinations use in psychiatric scenarios, helping you recognize safety-based nursing responses under pressure.
home.study.pathoDesc: Understand disease processes at the cellular and molecular level, explained in clinical context. When you know why a condition produces specific signs and symptoms, you can reason through unfamiliar exam questions instead of relying on memorization alone.
home.study.pharmDesc: Study drug classes organized by mechanism of action, with emphasis on side effect profiles, contraindications, and high-alert medications. Content is designed to mirror the cognitive patterns tested on nursing licensure examinations, including dosage calculation errors and medication safety scenarios that appear frequently.
home.study.priorityDesc: Practice who-do-you-see-first scenarios, delegation decision-making, and the safety-based frameworks that underpin every prioritization question on nursing examinations. Learn to distinguish acute findings from expected findings and apply assignment logic confidently under timed conditions.
home.study.subtext: Structured from foundational concepts to advanced clinical judgment. Whether you are in your first semester or preparing for your licensing exam, the content meets you where you are.
home.study.subtitle: Every topic is built around the clinical reasoning patterns tested on nursing licensure examinations. No filler content. No outdated material. Every lesson connects pathophysiology to the decision-making frameworks that examiners use to write questions.
home.studyNextPreview.footerAfterLink: برای ثبت تمرین؛ هر زمان برای Study Next کامل و بانک سوال ارتقا دهید.

home.studyNextPreview.title: مرحله مطالعه بعدی را ببینید
home.tools.desc: Med math calculator, lab value interpreter, and drug reference.
home.tools.title: Clinical Tools
home.why.analyticsDesc: Track your proficiency by body system, identify weak areas with precision, and focus your study time where it matters most. Pre-test and post-test comparison data shows exactly how your clinical reasoning is improving over time.
home.why.heading: Why Students Choose NurseNest
home.why.judgmentDesc: Content is organized around the cognitive patterns tested on nursing licensure examinations: recognizing cues, analyzing hypotheses, prioritizing actions, and evaluating outcomes. This mirrors the clinical judgment framework that modern exams are designed to measure.
home.why.noFillerDesc: Every lesson covers high-yield concepts that align with the content domains tested on nursing licensure examinations. No obscure topics, no outdated protocols, no wasted study sessions. Study what will actually be assessed.
home.why.progressionDesc: Start with foundational pathophysiology and build toward complex case analysis. Each lesson connects to related topics so concepts reinforce each other instead of existing in isolation, taking you from beginner to advanced systematically.
home.why.rationaleDesc: Every question, lesson, and simulation explains the clinical reasoning behind the correct answer. You learn why an option is right and why the others are wrong, building the judgment patterns that examiners assess on licensing examinations.
home.why.simulationDesc: Work through branching patient scenarios where your decisions determine outcomes. Practice clinical reasoning in a risk-free environment before your exam or your first shift, with detailed feedback on every decision point.
home.why.subtitle: Most study tools hand you a question and an answer key. NurseNest teaches you how to reason through clinical problems the way licensing exams expect you to, so unfamiliar questions stop being intimidating.

learner.exams.page.subscriberIntro: اجراهای زمان‌بندی‌شده NCLEX-RN و NCLEX-PN ایالات متحده از استخر اشتراک شما استفاده می‌کنند. پاسخ‌ها تا زمانی که ارسال نکنید پنهان می‌مانند. در پایان یک امتیاز دریافت می کنید (بدون منطق بین آیتم ها، همان سرعت روز آزمون).

learner.exams.preset.fullSection.intro: قرعه‌کشی‌هایی با موضوعات مختلط با باندهای دشواری متعادل، سپس درهم‌آمیزی نهایی. آزمون‌های کامل RN بر اولویت‌بندی، مشتریان ناپایدار و تفویض اختیار تأکید دارند. آزمون‌های کامل PN بر مشتریان پایدار، وظایف و دامنه تأکید دارند. مشترکین RN/NP همچنان می‌توانند تمرین کامل PN را از طریق نردبان ردیف باز کنند.
learner.exams.preset.fullSection.title: تمرین کامل NCLEX ({{count}} سوال)

learner.exams.preset.pnMixed.title: PN تمرین ترکیبی ({{count}} سوال)
learner.exams.preset.rnFull.body: تمرین کامل با استفاده از استخر از پیش تعیین شده RN ایالات متحده برای اشتراک خود.
learner.exams.preset.rnFull.title: NCLEX-RN کامل است
learner.exams.preset.rnMixed.body: قرعه کشی به هم ریخته از موارد برچسب گذاری شده با دسته از پیش تعیین شده RN. در دسترس مشترکین ردیف RN.

learner.qbank.empty.default.body: موضوع دیگری را امتحان کنید، فیلترها را پاک کنید یا بازخوانی کنید. اگر اخیرا مشترک شده اید، کمی صبر کنید و دوباره امتحان کنید.

learner.qbank.empty.entitlementExcludesA: سوالات منتشر شده وجود دارد، اما هیچ کدام با کشور و سطح حساب شما مطابقت ندارند. کشور و برنامه نمایه خود را بررسی کنید یا با پشتیبانی تماس بگیرید.
هیچ سوالی با نمایه شما مطابقت ندارد
learner.qbank.empty.indeterminate.body: لیست به دلیلی غیر معمول خالی است. فیلترها را پاک کنید، سپس بازخوانی کنید. اگر ادامه داشت، با پشتیبانی زمانی که امتحان کردید تماس بگیرید.
learner.qbank.empty.indeterminate.title: سوالی در این دیدگاه وجود ندارد
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayNotOnPlan.bod: این مسیر در اشتراک شما گنجانده نشده است، بنابراین به عنوان فیلتر اعمال نشده است. فیلتر مسیر را پاک کنید یا ارتقا دهید. اگر لیست همچنان خالی است، دلیل زیر همچنان اعمال می‌شود.
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayNotOnPlan.tit: مسیری که در برنامه شما نیست
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayPoolEmpty.bod: اشتراک شما مواردی دارد، اما هیچ کدام برای این مسیر امتحان برچسب گذاری نشده اند. «همه موضوعات» را بدون فیلتر مسیر امتحان کنید یا مسیر دیگری را انتخاب کنید.
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayPoolEmpty.tit: چیزی برای این مسیر در استخر شما نیست
learner.qbank.empty.topicNoMatch.body: فیلتر موضوع دقیقاً با برچسب پایگاه داده مطابقت دارد. موضوع دیگری را از منو یا «همه موضوعات» انتخاب کنید.
learner.qbank.empty.topicNoMatch.title: سوالی برای این برچسب موضوع وجود ندارد
learner.qbank.empty.unknownPathway.body: «{{pathwayId}}» یک شناسه مسیر شناخته شده نیست، بنابراین بانک به آن محدود نشده است. فیلتر مسیر را بردارید یا مسیری را از هاب خود انتخاب کنید.
learner.qbank.empty.unknownPathway.title: فیلتر مسیر ناشناخته

learner.qbank.notice.topicRelaxed: مطابقت دقیقی برای موضوع "{{topic}}" وجود ندارد. به جای آن، سؤالاتی برای مسیر شما نشان داده می شود. از منوی موضوع برای محدود کردن بیشتر استفاده کنید.
learner.qbank.notice.weakLowConfidence: نگاشت موضوع با ناحیه ضعیف در حال حاضر اعتماد پایینی دارد. به درجه بندی موارد ادامه دهید و تراز موضوعات را سخت تر می کنیم.
learner.qbank.notice.weakUnavailable: حالت ناحیه ضعیف ابتدا به چند مورد درجه بندی شده نیاز دارد. ما یک مجموعه مختلط را نشان می دهیم تا زمانی که دفتر کل موضوع شما پر شود.
learner.qbank.pickTopic.body: یک موضوع را از منوی زیر برای شروع یک مسابقه مبتنی بر موضوع انتخاب کنید (ابتدا موارد اخیر).
learner.qbank.pickTopic.title: موضوعی را انتخاب کنید
learner.qbank.preset.pathwayMixed: آزمون ترکیبی مسیر (تصادفی، همتراز با امتحان)
learner.qbank.ui.batchProgressNote: پیشرفت این دسته در مرورگر شما ذخیره می شود تا بتوانید بازخوانی کنید و ادامه دهید. حالت‌های تصادفی و ترکیبی سؤالاتی را که قبلاً در این جلسه دیده‌اید، در هنگام بارگذاری بیشتر حذف نمی‌کنند. امتیاز دهی روی سرور اجرا می شود. پاسخ ها به تنهایی در صفحه درجه بندی نمی شوند.
learner.qbank.ui.examShellHint: جلسه امتحانی: توضیحات تا زمانی که شما انتخاب نکنید پنهان می مانند، مانند سرعت کتاب بسته (فقط تمرین).
learner.qbank.ui.learningHigh: مطابقت قوی از فراداده این مورد. مراحل بعدی باید در همان مسیر موضوع باقی بماند.
learner.qbank.ui.learningLow: فقط مسیریابی تقریبی اگر نتایج خارج از هدف به نظر می رسند، بانک سؤال را باز کنید و فیلترها را تنظیم کنید.
learner.qbank.ui.learningMedium: مسیریابی از موضوع سوال؛ اگر بانک احساس می کند گسترده است، با فیلترها یا صف بررسی اولویت شما تنگ است.
learner.qbank.ui.reviewFlashcards: فلش کارت ها را مرور کنید
learner.qbank.ui.sessionLineIdle: برای پیگیری این جلسه پاسخ دهید و بررسی کنید
learner.questions.howTo.footer: موارد ممتاز و دلایل منطقی برای استفاده مشترکان فردی است. کپی کردن روی صفحه محدود است. از یادداشت های من برای متنی که می توانید چاپ یا صادر کنید استفاده کنید.
learner.questions.howTo.integratedLoop: Misses here feed your weak-topic signals and can surface matched pathway lessons on the dashboard—keep lessons and bank practice in the same loop for faster gains.
learner.questions.howTo.li1: پاسخ ها را انتخاب کنید، سپس از بررسی پاسخ برای بازخورد فوری استفاده کنید.
learner.questions.howTo.li2: زمانی که می‌خواهید تمرین‌های زمان‌بندی‌شده و کاملی داشته باشید، با آزمون‌های تمرینی همراه شوید.
learner.questions.howTo.li3a: برای یک جلسه بانکی بیشتر شبیه امتحان (توضیحات پنهان تا زمانی که شرکت کنید)، اضافه کنید
learner.questions.howTo.li3b: به آدرس این صفحه یا استفاده کنید

learner.questions.subtitle.locked: پس از هر مورد، فیلترهای موضوع، و بررسی مناسب جلسه، همراه با یک برنامه فعال، با دلایل منطقی تمرین کنید.
learner.questions.subtitle.subscriber: به موارد MCQ و SATA پاسخ دهید، کار خود را با کلید رسمی بررسی کنید و منطق را بخوانید. از فیلتر موضوعی برای سوراخ کردن مناطق ضعیف استفاده کنید. پیشرفت این جلسه در مرورگر شما ذخیره می شود.

learner.readinessPage.catNone: No completed CAT-style practice tests yet. When you finish adaptive runs, we show accuracy here for context (the headline index still uses mocks and bank practice in its formula).
learner.readinessPage.catSummary: {n} completed CAT session(s). Average accuracy where scored: {pct}%.
learner.readinessPage.confidenceExplaine: Confidence reflects how much graded practice and mock history we have, not clinical certainty.
learner.readinessPage.consistencyNote: Mock score spread across attempts is folded into the mock factor when you have multiple exams — wide swings temper the headline index.
learner.readinessPage.formula.lessons: Lesson completion vs lessons available in your plan pool.
learner.readinessPage.formula.mocks: Recent mock exams (attempts with at least five scored items each).
learner.readinessPage.formula.practice: Accuracy on graded items from recent completed question-bank and exam sessions (your plan scope).
learner.readinessPage.formula.renormaliz: If a signal is missing (for example, no mocks yet), its weight is left out and the index is renormalized so you are not penalized with unexplained empty points.
learner.readinessPage.formula.topics: Topic error load from priority weak areas (ledger + sessions).
learner.readinessPage.indexFootnote: This number is a NurseNest readiness index (0–100), not a published passing probability.
This index blends pathway lesson progress, graded bank and mock performance, and topic load from your weak areas—so it reflects study and practice together, not isolated quiz scores.
Why this feels different from a question-only bank
learner.readinessPage.noGradedPractice: No graded bank items in recent completed sessions yet.
learner.readinessPage.noMocks: No mock attempts on file yet.
learner.readinessPage.section.catSub: Adaptive (CAT) practice tests you have completed — shown for transparency; the headline score above does not add a separate CAT weight.
learner.readinessPage.section.consistenc: Study streak and how mock variability affects the mock portion of your index.
learner.readinessPage.section.formulaSub: Matches the server-side blend in `computeReadiness` — weights apply when each signal qualifies.
learner.readinessPage.section.mocksSub: Newest scored mocks (same pool used in the mock factor when eligible).
learner.readinessPage.section.signalsSub: Point contributions after your data qualifies for each signal.
learner.readinessPage.section.weakSub: From unified topic performance. Use questions for targeted drills; lessons for structured review.
learner.readinessPage.streakCaption: Consecutive calendar days (UTC) with any study signal in the last 90 days.
learner.readinessPage.weakEmpty: No weak topics surfaced yet. Complete more graded questions so we can prioritize systems for you.
learner.reportCard.bankSessionsDetail: {correct} correct of {total} graded items across {sessions} sessions.
learner.reportCard.bankSessionsNa: No graded bank or exam-session items in your recent history yet. Complete a timed or tutor session to populate this.
learner.reportCard.loadFailed: We could not load your report card right now. Try again shortly.
learner.reportCard.mockNa: No scored mock attempts yet. Start a practice exam to see accuracy here.
learner.reportCard.mockTierNa: No mock attempts grouped by exam track yet.
learner.reportCard.next.catBody: Use full mocks for exam-length rehearsal, or the practice-test builder for adaptive (CAT) runs when your plan includes them.
learner.reportCard.next.lessonsNa: No in-progress lesson detected. Open lessons to continue your pathway.
learner.reportCard.next.questionsNa: Topic-level drill needs a bit more graded history. Open the bank and practice by system.
learner.reportCard.overallEmptyHint: Start with a few graded bank items or a mock — your report card will fill in as you go.
learner.reportCard.pathwaysNa: No pathway lesson progress recorded yet for your subscription scope.
learner.reportCard.practiceTestsNa: No completed practice tests yet. Finish a CAT or fixed-length test to see results here.
learner.reportCard.questionTierNa: Not enough recent graded session items to break down accuracy by question tier (RN / LPN / NP, etc.).
learner.reportCard.recentBankNa: No recent graded sessions with scored items.
learner.reportCard.section.mockTierSub: Groups your mock attempts by the exam’s pathway tier (RN, RPN/LPN, NP, allied).
learner.reportCard.section.mockTrendSub: Weekly average score on mocks when we have attempts in more than one week.
learner.reportCard.section.nextStepsSub: Based on your current progress and weak signals — not a guarantee of exam outcome.
learner.reportCard.section.overallSub: Bank accuracy uses graded items from recent completed sessions; mock accuracy uses scored full-exam attempts.
learner.reportCard.section.pathwaysSub: Lesson completion by pathway — separate from question-bank accuracy.
learner.reportCard.section.practiceTests: Adaptive (CAT) and other practice tests you have completed.
learner.reportCard.section.questionTierS: From your latest graded session items (bank questions), grouped by item tier.
learner.reportCard.section.recentBankSub: Latest completed sessions with scored items.
learner.reportCard.section.strongTopicsS: Topics with stronger accuracy where we have enough attempts to say so.
learner.reportCard.section.trendsSub: Short notes when we detect improving, declining, or stable patterns.
learner.reportCard.section.weakTopicsSub: Higher miss rates where we have graded evidence.
learner.reportCard.strongNa: No strong topics identified yet — keep practicing to build a clearer picture.
learner.reportCard.trendNa: Trend needs mock attempts in at least two different weeks. Keep testing — we will chart it when there is enough spread.
learner.reportCard.weakNa: No weak topics flagged yet from graded history.
learner.security.noPasswordAfter: with your email to set one.
learner.security.noPasswordBefore: This account does not use a NurseNest password yet. Use
learner.security.passwordPolicyHint: Use at least 8 characters with a letter and a number.
learner.security.resetBody: If you cannot remember your password, we will email a one-time link. The link expires after a short time. If you sign in with Google or another provider only, use this once to create a NurseNest password.
learner.security.section.passwordSub: Uses your current password. After a successful change you will be signed out here and should sign in again.
learner.security.section.resetSub: Same secure flow as “Forgot password” on the sign-in page.
learner.security.sessionExplain: NurseNest keeps you signed in with an encrypted browser cookie (about {{days}} days). It is not shared with other sites. We do not show a list of devices or browsers.
learner.security.sessionOtherDevices: After you change your password, this browser signs out automatically. On any other device or browser, open NurseNest and choose Sign out if you no longer want that session, or simply sign in again when the old session expires.
learner.security.trouble1: Confirm you are using the same email you registered with, or your username if you set one.
learner.security.trouble2: Try an incognito or private window to rule out extensions or old cookies.
learner.security.trouble3: Request a new reset link from the forgot-password page; older links stop working after use or when they expire.
learner.security.trouble4: Make sure your device clock is roughly correct; extreme skew can break some secure flows.
learner.sessionInsight.focusTopicDrill: When you are ready, a short question drill on {{topic}} matches what this run showed.

pages.alliedHealthHub.footerNursingPathw: Looking for NCLEX or NP instead? Browse nursing and advanced practice pathways.
pages.alliedHealthHub.heroH1: Certification prep for your discipline, not a generic nursing page
pages.alliedHealthHub.heroTestimonial: Allied candidates tell us the stems feel closer to registry exams than random quiz banks.

pages.pricing.faq.0.a: Plans are sold by pathway tier (for example RN, PN, NP, or Allied) and country. After purchase, that tier unlocks the matching question bank, lessons, and practice modes for that registration context.
pages.pricing.faq.1.a: Yes. NurseNest uses Stripe for checkout and subscription management. You can update payment methods and cancel from the billing portal.
pages.pricing.faq.2.a: Refunds follow the subscription and refund policy linked at checkout. If you have a billing issue, contact support with your account email.
pages.pricing.faq.3.a: Your bank stays scoped to the tier you bought. Switching tracks usually means a new plan that matches the new pathway.
pages.pricing.faq.3.q: Can I change exam tracks later?
pages.pricing.faq.4.a: Use timed sets when your accuracy is stable on topics, then full practice exams for pacing. The app highlights weak categories so your next block targets risk, not comfort.

tools.transfusionSafety.intro: Tap the best immediate action. Rationales summarize what to watch for on exams and on the unit.
tools.transfusionSafety.metaDescription: Practice identifying transfusion reactions and first-line nursing responses.
tools.transfusionSafety.opt2a: Rising free hemoglobin or plasma hemoglobin with decreasing haptoglobin (with clinical correlation).
tools.transfusionSafety.opt2b: Isolated platelet count drop without other signs.
tools.transfusionSafety.optA: Stop the transfusion, keep IV access with normal saline, and notify the provider and blood bank per protocol.
tools.transfusionSafety.optB: Slow the infusion to 50 mL/hr and continue monitoring.
tools.transfusionSafety.optC: Give antipyretics and continue the transfusion if vital signs stabilize.
tools.transfusionSafety.q1: A patient develops fever, chills, and flank pain 45 minutes into a red blood cell transfusion. What is your priority?
tools.transfusionSafety.q2: You suspect acute hemolytic transfusion reaction. Which lab pair best supports hemolysis in this context?
tools.transfusionSafety.rationale1: Febrile or hemolytic suspicion requires stopping the blood product, maintaining patent IV access with saline, monitoring, and escalation—before restarting anything.
tools.transfusionSafety.rationale2: Hemolysis releases free hemoglobin and consumes haptoglobin; always interpret with the clinical picture and institutional transfusion reaction pathway.
tools.transfusionSafety.title: Transfusion safety
